hypothesis: `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` has now taken the frontier at `K=16`, `K=8`, `K=4`, and `K=2`; testing the same fixed recipe at `K=1` determines whether this family can cover the full low-K Pareto frontier and potentially beat the current `K=1` anchor from `lowrank_two_stage_residual`.
summary: Run one controlled parameter-only experiment at `K=1` with `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EF=12`, `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed so `K` is the only changed variable.
self_review: This remains the strongest single-variable continuation. The family has produced four consecutive frontier wins as K decreased, so the highest-value next question is whether that dominance extends all the way to `K=1` before returning to broader architecture exploration.
notes_to_memory: Round 69 tests whether `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual`, now on the frontier at `K=16`, `K=8`, `K=4`, and `K=2`, can extend to the extreme endpoint at `K=1` under the same fixed recipe.
Keep architecture and recipe fixed (`EF=12`, `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`) so `K` is the only changing dimension.
Success criterion is beating the current `K=1` frontier anchor (`lowrank_two_stage_residual`, FVU `0.17282412946224213`) and confirming whether this family spans the full low-K frontier.
next_hypotheses: If `K=1` wins, treat `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` as the dominant low-K family and shift focus back to mid-K/new-family search.
If `K=1` is close but slightly behind, consider one confirmatory rerun before concluding the family bottoms out at `K=2`.
If `K=1` clearly loses, keep `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` as strongest in the `K=2-16` band and resume new-family search for the `K=1` slot.
